notes: SHIP v1.14.221 — DEPLOY BUG-HUNT · INSTRUMENT (per HANDOFF-deploy-bug-hunt.md Ship 1, base .220). Instrument-first: capture the recurring Work→Deploy break with a stack trace so v1.14.222 fixes the real root cause instead of guessing. ★ MAJOR RECONCILIATION (deviation, reported): the handoff — written on a stale .219 base — asked for a NEW `phantom_errlog` (50-entry) + NEW window.onerror/unhandledrejection handlers. Those ALREADY EXIST as `phantom_crash_log` (DCT-04 IIFE @16000-16047: window.onerror + unhandledrejection + logCrash + #crash-banner + version-keyed auto-clear + phantomViewCrashLog viewer — the .215 crash-log hardening ship). Building a parallel log would violate 'one door per feature' and double-register window.onerror. So this ship EXTENDS the existing system instead of duplicating it. Also: the handoff targeted .220 for INSTRUMENT, but .220 was already live (3D rack parity) — re-anchored INSTRUMENT→.221, FIX→.222 (owner OK'd 'go as .221'). CHANGES: (1) NEW `phantom_logErr(ctx, err)` global (after phantomViewCrashLog @16066) — the genuinely-missing piece: the existing handler only sees UNCAUGHT errors, but the deploy break is CAUGHT-and-swallowed, so window.onerror never fires. phantom_logErr writes a caught error (adds ctx + 500-char stack, type:'caught') into the SAME phantom_crash_log ring (30-cap, newest-first unshift, version-stamped). Self-contained + fully try-wrapped (can never crash its caller); skips IGNORE_PATTERNS on purpose (hand-instrumented catches are deliberate records); also console.warns for live Safari-devtools visibility. (2) 12 DEPLOY-PATH silent catches converted to logging catches — NO control-flow change (still catch, still degrade): 17212 showWorkTab:deploy [THE chokepoint], 17552/17555/17556/17560 cmd_render rollup reads, 18370 blocker_deployPicker:rollup, 18384 blocker_rackPicker:getById, 18387 blocker_rackPicker:loadRacks, 24662 deploy_opsHost:hostResolve, 24850 deploy_detailTool:render [already console.warns → ADDED ring write, warn kept], 26897 mscope_open:jobsnapProbe, 26913 mscope_loadMaster:reject [already console.errors → ADDED ring write beside it]. (3) VISIBLE FALLBACK CARD in the 17212 chokepoint catch — after logging, writes a 'DEPLOY FAILED TO RENDER — see SYS → ERRORS' card + RETRY button into the deploy_opsHost() return value (NEVER hardcoded #wk-deploy — r5 legacy-divergence trap), guarded redesign_isOn() so legacy's failure path stays byte-identical. A tech never stares at a dead stub again. (4) SYS → ERRORS surface: a body.rd-gated `ERRORS (n)` row in #hdr-agg-panel (mirrors #hdr-agg-profile-row gating exactly: base display:none + body.rd flex) → opens a new #rd-errors-sheet (reuses the .rd-sheet primitive) listing the ring newest-first with COPY ALL (clipboard + prompt() fallback, run in the tap gesture) + CLEAR (confirm → removeItem + hide banner + re-render). Count refreshes on panel-open via a redesign_isOn()-guarded errors_refreshCount() hook in hdr_aggToggle (legacy stays inert). DEFERRED (out of scope, reported): the earliest-boot-window capture gap (the existing handler registers at 16024, after a ~4,450-line boot window) — orthogonal to the deploy bug (which is a CAUGHT error no window.onerror sees regardless of timing) and adds clobber/double-log risk; not built this ship. EXISTING crash pipeline (logCrash / window.onerror / #crash-banner / phantomViewCrashLog / auto-clear) left BYTE-FOR-BYTE untouched → the capture layer's legacy behavior is unchanged. ADVERSARIAL REVIEW (3 refute-biased lenses, per-finding verified): correctness + reconciliation clean; ONE low finding CONFIRMED + FIXED — phantom_logErr's VISIBLE #crash-banner mutation is now redesign_isOn()-gated so ?legacy=1 is STRICTLY byte-identical on caught-error paths (e.g. a legacy master-load reject via mscope no longer raises the shared banner). The ring write + console.warn still run in BOTH houses (capture intact); the existing logCrash still drives the banner for UNCAUGHT errors in legacy, unchanged. Gates: node --check sw.js + 4 inline HTML blocks · CSS brace balance · CRLF preserved · three-stamp lockstep .220→.221 · phantom_logErr==1, ERRORS row/sheet gated, no hardcoded wk-deploy in the fallback, no second window.onerror= . ROLLBACK: pure additive (1 fn + sheet fns + 1 row + 1 sheet + 12 one-line catch fills + 8 CSS lines); git revert clean. DEVICE-VERIFY (COMBINED .220 + .221, hard stop): remove+re-add PWA, confirm v1.14.221, then — [A] .221 INSTRUMENT: (1) SYS pill → panel shows an ERRORS row with a count (NONE when clean); (2) tap it → ERRORS sheet lists entries (or 'No errors recorded'), COPY ALL copies to clipboard + toast, CLEAR empties it; (3) work the deploy path the way it normally breaks (Deploy card → + NEW scope picker → LIST → deploy detail → rack detail incl. FLAT↔3D → back-nav in/out repeatedly); (4) IF it breaks: you now get either a 'DEPLOY FAILED TO RENDER' card with RETRY (not a dead stub) AND/OR a red crash-banner — open SYS → ERRORS → COPY ALL → paste into chat (that stack trace is what .222 fixes); note whether force-close+reopen clears it (state-corruption vs code-path); (5) ?legacy=1 → NO errors row in the SYS panel, deploy failure path unchanged, byte-identical. [B] .220 3D PARITY (still owed): desktop 3D = glowing multi-color rack inside a lit steel cabinet (not flat floating tiles); phone matches MOCKUP-rack-threejs.html; orbit + pinch + MINI scrubber; tap tray → correct sheet; no orphan GPU on toggle/nav-away.
